methods.description: This method describes the cultivation of two rice genotypes, Nipponbare (NPB) and its mutant high-temperature susceptible (HTS), and their treatment with NAA or AOPP followed by heat stress to study various physiological responses.

steps.description: Two rice genotypes, namely the Nipponbare (NPB) and its mutant high-temperature susceptible (HTS) genotypes, were used in this study.
steps.searchable_summary: select rice genotypes NPB and HTS for study

steps.description: Rice seeds were soaked in running water for 48 h, then sprout at 37°C for 24 h.
About 20 grains were sown in each pot, and then thinned to four plants when the fifth leaf was emerging.
steps.searchable_summary: sow and thin rice plants in pots

steps.description: The rice plants were cultivated in the greenhouse with an automatic temperature control system to control the temperature until anthesis.
steps.searchable_summary: cultivate rice plants in controlled greenhouse

steps.description: At anthesis, the flowering spikelets were immediately sprayed with different concentrations of NAA or AOPP.
steps.searchable_summary: spray spikelets with NAA or AOPP

steps.description: About 5-10 min later, they were subjected to heat stress of 40°C for 2 h.
steps.searchable_summary: apply heat stress to treated spikelets

steps.description: The flowering spikelets in the middle of the panicle were labeled with a marking pen after the heat stress ended.

methods.method_name: Pollen Germination and Tube Growth Measurement
methods.description: This method measures pollen germination and pollen tube growth in the pistil after heat stress treatment.

steps.description: More than 50 flowering spikelets were collected after the heat stress treatment.
The pollinated stigmas were fixed in Carnoy's fixing reagent.
The samples were then washed with purified water, incubated in 10 mol·L − 1 NaOH for 6-10 min at 56°C, and then stained in a 0.1% (w/v) aniline blue solution for 12 h.
The pollen tube germination and growth in the pistil were observed and photographed at 350 nm with a fluorescence microscope.

methods.description: This method involves extracting RNA from pistils, converting it to cDNA, and performing quantitative PCR to analyze gene expression levels.

steps.description: Pistil from the heat-stress treated plants were frozen in liquid nitrogen and stored at − 80°C for 2 h.
Total RNA was extracted from 10 mg of pistil using the TRI pure reagent.
RNA was converted into first-strand cDNA using the Rever Tra Ace qPCR RT Master Mix using oligo (dT) as the primer.
inputs: Rever Tra Ace qPCR RT Master Mix

steps.description: The resultant cDNA was used as a template for quantitative PCR amplification using SYBR Green I as a fluorescent reporter.
steps.searchable_summary: perform qPCR with SYBR Green I

steps.description: The 2 −ΔΔCT method was used to determine the relative gene transcript levels with the mean value of triplicate experiments.
